array.string:
LOCK_FILE="{{ARTHA_ROOT}}/.artha-decrypted" VAULT_PY="{{ARTHA_ROOT}}/scripts/vault.py" PYTHON="${HOME}/.artha-venvs/.venv/bin/python" WATCHDOG_LOG="/tmp/artha-watchdog.log" TIMESTAMP=$(date -Iseconds)
# Only act if lock file exists (session was started) if [[ -f "${LOCK_FILE}" ]]; then
ARTHA_SESSION_ACTIVE=false
# Check TTL: if lock is older than 30 min, force-encrypt regardless LOCK_AGE=$(( $(date +%s) - $(stat -f %m "${LOCK_FILE}" 2>/dev/null || echo 0) )) VAULT_TTL=1800 # 30 min default (matches foundation.py LOCK_TTL) if [[ "${LOCK_AGE}" -gt "${VAULT_TTL}" ]]; then echo "[${TIMESTAMP}] WATCHDOG: Lock TTL exceeded (age: ${LOCK_AGE}s). Auto-encrypting." >> "${WATCHDOG_LOG}" if [[ -x "${PYTHON}" ]] && [[ -f "${VAULT_PY}" ]]; then "${PYTHON}" "${VAULT_PY}" encrypt >> "${WATCHDOG_LOG}" 2>&1 echo "[${TIMESTAMP}] WATCHDOG: TTL auto-encrypt complete." >> "${WATCHDOG_LOG}" fi exit 0 fi
# Claude Code — narrow match to avoid false positives pgrep -f "claude.*Artha" > /dev/null 2>&1 && ARTHA_SESSION_ACTIVE=true
# Gemini CLI — narrow: gemini process working in Artha dir pgrep -f "gemini.*Artha" > /dev/null 2>&1 && ARTHA_SESSION_ACTIVE=true
# VS Code (Copilot) — VS Code with Artha folder open pgrep -f "Code.*Artha" > /dev/null 2>&1 && ARTHA_SESSION_ACTIVE=true
if [[ "${ARTHA_SESSION_ACTIVE}" == "false" ]]; then # No active AI session detected — encrypt stale plaintext echo "[${TIMESTAMP}] WATCHDOG: Lock file found, no active AI session. Auto-encrypting." >> "${WATCHDOG_LOG}" if [[ -x "${PYTHON}" ]] && [[ -f "${VAULT_PY}" ]]; then "${PYTHON}" "${VAULT_PY}" encrypt >> "${WATCHDOG_LOG}" 2>&1 echo "[${TIMESTAMP}] WATCHDOG: Auto-encrypt complete." >> "${WATCHDOG_LOG}" else echo "[${TIMESTAMP}] WATCHDOG: ERROR — vault.py or python not found." >> "${WATCHDOG_LOG}" fi else echo "[${TIMESTAMP}] WATCHDOG: Active AI session detected. Vault stays open." >> "${WATCHDOG_LOG}" fi fi
